food_subgroup: Chicken-based sandwich meal with salad components and dessert cookie

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for an avocado Caesar chicken sandwich meal with cookie. FOODON_00002473 (chicken sandwich food product) used as closest parent. Avocado, Caesar dressing, and cookie components are not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Chicken sandwich) used as closest parent. The full meal set including avocado, Caesar dressing, and cookie side is not individually represented in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this composite meal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical meal set consisting of an avocado Caesar chicken sandwich or wrap (approximately 350 g) plus one cookie (approximately 70 g), totalling around 420 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken Caesar Sandwich with Avocado (composite estimate from SR Legacy and Foundation Foods components: grilled chicken breast, avocado, Caesar dressing, wheat bread, lettuce, cookie)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exists for this exact meal. Values are estimated by component-weighted composition: grilled chicken breast (USDA SR Legacy #05064, ~120 g), avocado (USDA SR Legacy #09037, ~40 g), Caesar dressing (USDA SR Legacy #04135, ~30 g), wheat sub roll (USDA SR Legacy #18413, ~120 g), romaine lettuce (USDA SR Legacy #11251, ~20 g), and a standard chocolate chip cookie (USDA SR Legacy #18158, ~70 g). Per-100g values are derived from the weighted average of the full 420 g meal set. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021;114(5):1625-1632. Analogous estimate based on wheat sub roll / sandwich GI ~47–50 and mixed chicken-avocado filling lowering overall GI.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal. GI estimated at 48 based on: (1) wheat sub roll GI ~47 (University of Sydney GI Database, white wheat bread analogues); (2) protein and fat from chicken breast and avocado attenuate postprandial glucose response; (3) cookie component (~70 g, GI ~55 for standard chocolate chip cookie per Sydney GI Database) contributes to overall meal GI. Weighted meal GI estimated at 48, classified as low. GL per serving = (48 × 26.5 × 4.20) / 100 = 53.5 g carb per serving × 0.48 ≈ 29.8. Atkinson et al. 2021 used as primary GI reference framework.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; full meal set delivers an estimated 1764 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that one meal set provides approximately 77% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association; requesting reduced-sodium dressing and avoiding added salt is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load per serving approximately 29.8 g; refined wheat bread and cookie contribute rapidly digestible carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that the full meal set carries a glycemic load of approximately 30, which is above the low-GL threshold of 20; choosing a wholegrain wrap, omitting the cookie, or halving the portion can meaningfully reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 2.8 g per 100 g; Caesar dressing and cookie contribute saturated fat and sodium; avocado provides cardioprotective monounsaturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, the avocado component provides beneficial monounsaturated fatty acids, but the Caesar dressing and cookie elevate saturated fat and sodium; requesting light dressing and omitting the cookie reduces the cardiovascular risk profile of this meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Avocado Caesar Chicken with Cookie Meal is a Western fast-casual meal set sold in Singapore, notably at Subway outlets and mix-and-match food stalls. The meal typically consists of a chicken Caesar sandwich or wrap filled with grilled or roasted chicken breast, sliced avocado, Caesar dressing, and romaine lettuce, served on a wheat sub roll or in a wheat wrap, accompanied by a cookie as a dessert side. The avocado adds a creamy, buttery texture and contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, while the Caesar dressing provides a tangy, umami-rich flavour profile. The cookie side is typically a soft-baked chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin variety. Nutritionally, the full meal set (~420 g) delivers approximately 1042 kcal, 56.7 g protein, 42.8 g fat, and 111.3 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 1764 mg per meal, which is a concern for hypertensive individuals. The glycemic load is moderate-to-high at approximately 30, driven by the refined wheat bread and cookie. The avocado content provides dietary fibre and potassium. This meal is suitable for active adults seeking a protein-rich lunch but requires portion awareness for individuals managing blood pressure, blood glucose, or cardiovascular risk. It is commonly ordered as a lunch or dinner meal in Singapore's Western fast-casual dining segment.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA SR Legacy and Foundation Foods data for individual ingredients (grilled chicken breast, avocado, Caesar dressing, wheat sub roll, romaine lettuce, cookie). GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for wheat bread and cookie components. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on a direct laboratory analysis of this specific meal.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component ingredients in USDA SR Legacy and the contribution from this meal is expected to be negligible; confirmed not reported for this composite. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data was available. The meal set weight of 420 g is an estimate based on typical Subway sub roll meal portions in Singapore. The cookie weight (~70 g) is estimated from standard Subway cookie serving size. Sodium is elevated primarily due to Caesar dressing and bread; individuals with hypertension should exercise caution. The halal flag is set to true based on Subway Singapore's halal certification status as of the data collection date, but consumers should verify current certification status directly with the restaurant.

improvement_actions_taken: Canonicalized food_name from 'Avo Caesar Chicken Cookie Meal' to 'Avocado Caesar Chicken with Cookie Meal'.

Estimated per_100g nutrient values using USDA SR Legacy component-weighted methodology for 420 g meal set.
Estimated GI at 48 using University of Sydney GI Database analogous values for wheat bread and cookie; documented estimation basis.

Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
